title: Who designed the Geofront
wikitext:
[]: Who designed the Geofront?             Quote Post by mike » Wed Sep 01, 2004 7:28 am With all of the Olympic coverage lately, many programs involving the Spanish architect Santiago Calatrava have popped up. Calatrava was the man who redesigned the grand Olympic stadium in Athens. He is now making the subway terminal for ground zero in Manhattan. Before that he designed the Alamillo brigde in Seville: If you look at the Director's cut version 24th episode just after Kaworu talks to the Seele Monoliths (14 minutes 22 seconds into the episode) you see the exact same bridge. It's also seen in EoE during Asuka's battle against the MPs (35mins 34 secs and later 36mins 46 secs). Looks like Gendo likes modern Spanish architecture. Calatrava's other works can be found in his website: http://www.calatrava.com

[]:             Quote Post by Asuka Langley » Wed Sep 01, 2004 9:12 am Woah, I had never really noticed that. Thanks for that site link, btw.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Sep 01, 2004 10:53 am Wow, nice find. Sorry to say I don't know anything about the GeoFront's design.

[]:             Quote Post by Kiel » Wed Sep 01, 2004 2:29 pm I can't remember which ep it was, but wasn't the geofront itself found, not built. As for the architecture of the buildings, you have a point :) It does look earily similar

[]:             Quote Post by Nephilim » Wed Sep 01, 2004 2:38 pm To make it easier for all you people... Here are the screen caps !!! :P Episode 24: EoE: EoE: All thanks to Nephilim, the screencap master!!!! 8)

[]:             Quote Post by belial11737 » Wed Sep 01, 2004 3:33 pm It is the same ...wow!!!! What a great find.....

Calatrava was the man who redesigned the grand Olympic stadium in Athens. He is now making the subway terminal for ground zero in Manhattan. Before that he designed the Alamillo brigde in Seville: If you look at the Director's cut version 24th episode just after Kaworu talks to the Seele Monoliths (14 minutes 22 seconds into the episode) you see the exact same bridge. It's also seen in EoE during Asuka's battle against the MPs (35mins 34 secs and later 36mins 46 secs). Looks like Gendo likes modern Spanish architecture. []:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Tue Oct 26, 2004 9:13 pm what do you mean by enlarged? there are some shots of Misato talking with hyuuga on the bridge...thats all... and it looks expensive...several million?

[]:             Quote Post by Gilgamesh » Tue Oct 26, 2004 9:24 pm sadsadshinji wrote: what do you mean by enlarged? there are some shots of Misato talking with hyuuga on the bridge...thats all... and it looks expensive...several million?

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Wed Oct 27, 2004 4:48 pm Gilgamesh wrote: sadsadshinji wrote: what do you mean by enlarged? there are some shots of Misato talking with hyuuga on the bridge...thats all... and it looks expensive...several million?

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Wed Oct 27, 2004 9:52 pm i dunno if he would appreciate the 20 pages...then again..some teachers are such asses... there are several programs with which you can take screen shots... its just that i dont have any... and i think the evas are a cool idea... maybe you can even talk about the geometry of the evas themselves?

[]:             Quote Post by Dark FireStar » Thu Oct 28, 2004 3:25 pm Do you think the geometry of the Evas' has a signifasant meaning?

[]: Who designed the Geofront?             Quote Post by mike » Wed Sep 01, 2004 7:28 am With all of the Olympic coverage lately, many programs involving the Spanish architect Santiago Calatrava have popped up. Calatrava was the man who redesigned the grand Olympic stadium in Athens. He is now making the subway terminal for ground zero in Manhattan. Before that he designed the Alamillo brigde in Seville: If you look at the Director's cut version 24th episode just after Kaworu talks to the Seele Monoliths (14 minutes 22 seconds into the episode) you see the exact same bridge. It's also seen in EoE during Asuka's battle against the MPs (35mins 34 secs and later 36mins 46 secs). Looks like Gendo likes modern Spanish architecture. Calatrava's other works can be found in his website: http://www.calatrava.com
